raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("Incidence of two canals in mandibular incisors")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the retrieved evidence, the incidence of two canals in mandibular incisors appears to range widely, aligning with previously discussed estimates ranging roughly from 20% to 41%. Studies do suggest significant variability in pulpal chamber morphology, supporting the notion that two canals might exist but remain somewhat unusual exceptions rather than normative features. Given this distribution pattern noted consistently across various investigations cited, my revised stance supports maintaining selection of Option C (\boxed{C}) as representing a reasonable estimate reflective real-world observations documented scientifically despite limitations inherent in sampling methods employed historically impacting absolute figures quoted differently now updated accordingly post-retrieval latest relevant studies reviewed comprehensively.
